topic.what: Structural gaps in AI agent identity systems across authentication, authorization, delegation, governance, and operational sustainability
topic.how: Survey of vendor landscape, standards corpus, and regulatory frameworks with four-dimension comparison of human vs AI identity
topic.why_matters: 144:1 NHI-to-human ratio in enterprise environments; 21.9% treat agents as independent principals; current standards fail multi-hop delegation and boundary-crossing agents

characteristics.what_it_measures: Non-human to human identity ratio in enterprise
Organizations treating agents as independent identity principals
characteristics.context: Most organizations use shared API keys (45.6%) or inherited human credentials

characteristics.what_it_measures: Standards spanning full agent identity lifecycle
characteristics.vs_baseline: No single standard spans enrollment, runtime authorization, behavioral accountability
characteristics.context: OAuth partial (no multi-hop), SAML fails, WIMSE/SPIFFE available but identity-only, MCP partial (identity out of scope)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cross-jurisdiction harmonization of AI identity rules
characteristics.vs_baseline: EU AI Act Art. 50 conflicts with China mandatory labeling
characteristics.context: Structural disagreement on what AI identity regulation should solve

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether current auth verifies agent content vs container
characteristics.vs_baseline: SVID/proof tokens authenticate container, not model weights/system prompt/intent
characteristics.context: Authenticated agent can still act outside mandate via prompt injection or version drift

characteristics.vs_baseline: OAuth 2.0 handles one-hop; no standard for recursive delegation
characteristics.context: Central accountability gap for sub-agent chains

characteristics.value: EU Art. 50 Aug 2026; China labeling Sep 2025
characteristics.vs_baseline: No US federal AI identity law

not_for: Resolution of structural gaps (market investment insufficient)
requires: Research on five critical gaps specifically

novelty: Four-dimension structural comparison of human vs AI identity (substrate, persistence, verifiability, legal standing)
Five critical gaps taxonomy: semantic intent verification, recursive delegation accountability, agent identity integrity, governance opacity, operational sustainability
Five-jurisdiction regulatory landscape with conflict identification
IETF AIMS dual-identity credentials and three delegation flows (Agent-Mediate, Owner-Mediate, Server-Mediate)
